matched_terms: module consolidation; merge; refactor; facade; compatibility; leg36; reuse; duplication; module manifest; import graph; consumer
Workflow: Semantic module consolidation and compatibility-preserving refactor workflow
Trigger: Before new substantive Lean code after governance closure, or whenever the user asks whether semantically related modules should be merged, compacted, facaded, or kept separate.
Required input: LEG10 module manifest; LEG11 reuse ledger; LEG5 consumption map; repo_inventory/raw_export modules/import_edges/symbol references; candidate file list; direct code spot-checks; current public/API and proof-dossier records.
Required updates: Update LEG36, code_audit findings, reuse_duplication_ledger, scratchpad refactor fields, proposed modules, duplicate-risk symbols, natural-layer placement, facade/compatibility plan, and regression metrics. Set downstream content phases to orange if this gate must precede them.
Exit rule: No Lean merge may close without old-module facade or explicit migration plan, import graph delta, declaration/consumer delta, local full-build evidence, and proof that semantic/provenance auditability is preserved or improved.

Workflow: Pre-Implementation Phase Check / semantic thought experiment
Trigger: User asks to check the next semantically connected phases in advance, as a Gedankenexperiment, before implementation; also applicable before a risky multi-phase refactor or derived-only bridge sequence.
Required input: Current active cursor; phase table; scratchpad; object register and proof dossiers; module implementation manifest; reuse/duplication ledger; consumption map; static findings; last user-reported green build when available; requested semantic window or the next contiguous phase cluster inferred from dependencies.
Required updates: For every inspected phase, create/update a pre-implementation check record and mark Y if the phase is implementable under current dependencies, or R if a dependency, object, proof route, module seam, source, or consumer-contract gap is missing. Add newly discovered problems to Static code findings. For every R record, generate one or more upstream addressing phases or a documented merge/backjump target before the blocked phase; do not set the blocked phase active until those addressing phases are closed.
Exit rule: The check closes only when every phase in the semantic window has a Y/R result, each R result has a concrete addressing phase or documented merge target, generated phases have object-link policy and scratchpad stubs, and all changed records carry the current tool version/timestamp. Lean-code data timestamp remains unchanged unless Lean files or Lean inventory are changed.

Workflow: Secondary long-term code-documentation and derivation-geometry workflow
Trigger: A request proposes documenting the existing codebase, analyzing the geometry of the formal derivation graph, studying bottlenecks/dominators/source-sink paths, or reflecting on open-system emergence in the formalization topology.
Required input: Stable generated repo inventory; object proof dossiers; declaration consumption classification; active not-before gates; explicit derived-only/comparison-only boundary.
Required updates: Register the goal as secondary if prerequisites are not closed. If active later, update coverage/dominators/layer/path diagnostics and link them to objects/modules without changing CNNA generative inputs.
Exit rule: Outputs are documentation, diagnostics, or comparison reports only. They may guide audits and refactors, but cannot become mathematical or physical premises without a separate CNNA-derived proof path.

matched_terms: refactor; reuse; duplication; module manifest; consumer
Trigger: Before starting any active phase after a user-reported green build, and before changing Lean code or closing an infrastructure gate.
Required input: active phase_id; current phase dependencies; scratchpad preparation fields; Section 2 static findings with Priority/Issue status/Addressing phase; module manifest; reuse/duplication audit; object proof dossiers when relevant.
Required updates: State whether the active phase is feasible as infrastructure-only, refactor, or Lean implementation. If a blocking issue is found, add or update a Static code finding row with R/Y/G priority, status, and addressing phase; set or keep the phase red/yellow accordingly and backjump to the natural source layer rather than patching a consumer.
Exit rule: Implementation may start only after the active phase has a positive feasibility result or a precisely localized missing bridge. New findings may not remain prose-only; they must be represented in Section 2 with a phase assignment and priority.

matched_terms: refactor; module manifest; import graph; consumer
Trigger: Any phase moves declarations, splits a module, introduces wrappers/aliases, quarantines legacy gates, or changes imports.
Required input: Module manifest; declaration list or family; current and target imports; consumer list; local build evidence plan.
Required updates: Fill refactor_regression_protocol and refactor_migration_metrics. Record atomically delivered package boundary, declarations migrated count, import graph delta, consumer breakage audit, and local full-build status source.
Exit rule: A refactor phase closes only after the delivered package has local full-build evidence. Intermediate wrapper coexistence is allowed; deleting legacy/status material before quarantine closure is not allowed.

Trigger: Before any operational Lean implementation starts, whenever a computable path, ExecComplex bridge, generated matrix/operator, recurrence, determinant, inverse candidate, or smoke-test route is introduced or modified.
Required input: active phase_id; target operational declarations; at least one concrete finite instance or an explicit infrastructure-only justification; expected executable witness; current mathlib imports and known computability risks.
Required updates: Fill concrete_instance_traversal, computable_path_not_skeleton_status, expected_executable_witness, mathlib_complication_log, noncomputable_boundary_audit, and mathlib_mitigation_or_refactor_layer in the scratchpad. Record whether the instance reaches the intended output object or where it stops.
Exit rule: Implementation may start only if the phase either has a concrete instance path through the computable code or is explicitly infrastructure-only. Any mathlib noncomputable leak must be isolated in an allowed mirror/non-operational layer or backjumped to the natural source layer; it cannot silently enter the operational path.

Trigger: Before implementing or promoting any operational computable path, arithmetic wrapper, generator extension, lattice test, or refactor that claims computation is preserved.
Required input: Canonical generator/branching API; finite instance parameters; L_max/branch-mode rows; expected executable witness; optional sentinel/comparison rows; mathlib/noncomputable risk log.
Required updates: Fill O07 or a consumer of O07; update concrete_instance_traversal, expected_executable_witness, mathlib_complication_log, and no-go guardrails. Separate generic rows from sentinel rows.
Exit rule: The phase cannot close on hand-picked CM/Eisenstein/Gauss examples alone. At least one generic row family must traverse the actual computable path, or a documented failure/backjump is required.

Trigger: When Phase 1.1.2 or the first real code-change/refactor package closes.
Required input: Closed refactor report; list of governance gates used; mistakes prevented; time/complexity overhead; upcoming Phase 3+ structure.
Required updates: Fill governance_overhead_review. Decide whether the current depth remains necessary or whether later phases should use a flatter structure with the same global guardrails.
Exit rule: Do not remove global no-go rules. Only the phase granularity may be flattened, and only after a recorded retrospective.

Trigger: Whenever a phase is added, split, merged, moved, or whenever an object Phase field changes.
Required input: Phase hierarchy; object register; direct object ownership map; child-phase scope; moved/audit/closeout status.
Required updates: Update phase Object link policy, Object link explanation, Child object scope, object Phase fields, proof dossiers and phase table generator if needed.
Exit rule: The generated phase table may not contain an unexplained empty Linked objects cell. Direct ownership is never inferred from child scope; child-scope summaries are display-only.

Trigger: Every phase preparation or status change.
Required input: phase_type; phase status; target code/object role; active scratchpad.
Required updates: Fill phase_type and phase_type_specific_contract. For refactor phases fill migration/regression fields; for derivation phases fill proof/tactic/mathlib fields; for comparison phases fill derived-only boundary; for infrastructure phases justify absence of operational witness.
Exit rule: A phase cannot become active unless its phase_type-specific evidence is prepared. Not-applicable filler is acceptable only with an explicit infrastructure-only or comparison-only justification.
